seed_problem: Frontier-CS polyomino/area packing: pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (reflections allowed); checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
mutation: input+goal (composite): replace the flat board with a per-cell regolith-DEPTH field (0 = unbuildable bedrock) and give each polyomino bay type a fixed scientific VALUE w_t, a shielding threshold r_t (every covered cell must have depth >= r_t), and bounded stock c_t. Change the objective from max covered AREA to max total per-module VALUE, turning it into a weighted bounded-knapsack polyomino packing where high-value bays compete for scarce deep-regolith ground (NP-hard, value- and threshold-sensitive).
title: Lunar Habitat: Regolith-Shielded Module Bay Packing
strategies: single-type baseline: anchored greedy using only bay type 0 up to its (generous) stock, blind to value and depth (the calibration point)
value-first greedy: row-major scan; at each buildable cell install, over all in-stock types/orientations anchored there whose shielding threshold is met, the placement of maximum module value w_t

density local search: multi-restart randomized cell orders, trying every sub-cell of every orientation as the landing cell and selecting by value-per-area w_t/s_t so high-value deep bays are packed onto scarce deep-regolith patches; keep the best total value
knapsack-repair: fill with small low-shielding bays, then re-pack fragmented deep-regolith pockets with scarce high-value high-shielding bays to lift total value
